sanskrit: सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः शृणु मे परमं वचः। इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम्॥
transliteration: sarva-guhyatamaṃ bhūyaḥ śṛṇu me paramaṃ vacaḥ | iṣṭo 'si me dṛḍham iti tato vakṣyāmi te hitam ||
literal_meaning: Again, hear from Me the most secret, supreme statement; you are very dear to Me, therefore I shall speak what is beneficial for you.
contextual_meaning: Krishna signals a final, intimate disclosure—moving from philosophy to relationship.  The teaching now becomes purely relational: “Because you matter to Me, I will tell you the one thing that dissolves every other worry.”  Psychologically, it is the moment when the therapist leans forward and says, “I’m going to trust you with the core intervention that makes everything else work.”
psychological_state: Secure-attachment activation: the nervous system shifts from hyper-vigilance (battlefield) to social-engagement mode, allowing frontal-lobe receptivity instead of limbic reactivity.
trigger_scenario: You’re in therapy, stuck in analysis-paralysis over life choices; the therapist suddenly drops the theoretical frame, looks you in the eye, and says, “You’re important to me—stop trying to solve this alone and let me hold the weight with you.”
anchor_text: Because you are loved, here is the one secret that ends every fear.
